data.title: Kulicke and Soffa outlines $145M Q3 revenue guidance amid Southeast Asia slowdown and advanced product focus
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Kulicke and Soffa Industries (KLIC) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Fusen Chen announced the decision to discontinue the electronics assembly (EA) equipment business, citing the need to align with long-term technology trends and enhance margins. The company will retain technology and aftermarket support for existing customers.
  • Chen emphasized prioritization of core areas, including Ball, Wedge, and Thermo-Compression technologies, as well as the Advanced Packaging Solutions (APS) and Advanced Dispense portfolios.
  • Chen highlighted a sequential increase in Ball Bonding utilization rates predominantly in the U.S. and China, resulting in a 38% improvement.
  • The company launched the ATPremier MEM Plus for stacked DRAM, aiming to address advanced memory packaging needs, and introduced a pin welding system for power semiconductor applications to support the EV and sustainable energy markets.
  • CFO Lester Wong reported $162 million in revenue for Q2 2025, with gross margins at 24.9% due to EA-related charges. Operating expenses totaled $125.1 million, including $86.6 million in EA wind-down costs. Excluding these, expenses were $76.5 million.
  • Wong disclosed a share repurchase program under which $21.3 million worth of shares were bought during the quarter.

Outlook

  • Management projects Q3 2025 revenue of $145 million, plus or minus $10 million, and gross margins of 46.5%.
  • Non-GAAP operating expenses are expected to be $68 million, plus or minus 2%. The company forecasts a GAAP EPS loss of $0.09 and a non-GAAP EPS gain of $0.05.
  • Chen noted that while Southeast Asia has experienced a slowdown, utilization improvements in Taiwan and China signal potential for capacity expansion. A sequential recovery in Q4 is anticipated.
  • Plans include leveraging advanced product lines like Vertical Wire and Thermo-Compression solutions to capitalize on memory and logic market opportunities.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q2 2025 was $162 million, down from $166.1 million in Q1 2025. Gross margins were impacted by EA-related charges, which totaled $38.6 million.
  • Operating expenses of $125.1 million included $8.8 million in restructuring charges and $39.8 million in impairment charges. Excluding these, expenses were $76.5 million.
  • The company repurchased over 500,000 shares for $21.3 million during the quarter.
  • Sequential order activity decreased in Southeast Asia but increased in China and Taiwan, reflecting diverging regional trends.

Q&A

  • Krish Sankar, TD Cowen, inquired about the Q3 slowdown. CEO Chen attributed it mainly to weakness in Southeast Asia automotive and industrial markets, while utilization rates improved in Taiwan and China.
  • Sankar asked about TCB progress. Chen stated their focus is on logic customers, with plans to grow in memory by 2026, supported by technological confidence.
  • Tom Diffely, D.A. Davidson, requested clarity on EA business metrics. CFO Wong detailed revenue of $25-$30 million annually, with gross profit of $7-$11 million and operating expenses between $20-$25 million.
  • Charles Shi, Needham & Co., asked about regional market dynamics. Chen explained higher utilization in Taiwan and China compared to Southeast Asia due to uncertainty in automotive industrial markets.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns over regional demand fluctuations, particularly in Southeast Asia, but showed interest in the company's growth in advanced packaging.
  • Management demonstrated confidence in long-term strategic positioning, emphasizing leadership in advanced technologies and market recovery potential. Chen’s acknowledgment of challenges in Southeast Asia reflected a cautious but optimistic outlook.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistent, focusing on recovery and leveraging emerging opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Q3 2025 was lower than Q2 due to macroeconomic challenges and regional demand hesitations.
  • Strategic focus shifted further toward advanced packaging solutions, discontinuing the EA business.
  • Analysts' attention moved from general market recovery to specific growth areas like Vertical Wire and TCB.
  • Management maintained confidence in long-term growth potential, despite near-term challenges.

Risks and Concerns

  • Macroeconomic uncertainty and trade dynamics are affecting customer confidence and orders, especially in Southeast Asia.
  • Potential delays in customer capital expenditures due to geopolitical tensions and tariff concerns.
  • Management highlighted mitigation through diversified regional exposure and a focus on advanced product lines.

Final Takeaway

Kulicke and Soffa Industries is navigating macroeconomic uncertainties by pivoting towards advanced packaging technologies and discontinuing its EA equipment business. With a projected Q3 2025 revenue of $145 million and a focus on strategic growth areas like Vertical Wire and Thermo-Compression, the company aims to strengthen its market positioning amidst regional demand fluctuations.

data.title: Macquarie initiates on CoreWeave with Neutral rating
data.description:
Macquarie on Wednesday initiated coverage of AI cloud reseller CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) with a Neutral rating and a price target of $56. 

CoreWeave's customizability for AI, along with its highly efficient platform has made it a disruptor in an industry historically dominated by major general-purpose cloud computing platforms, such as AWS, Google Cloud, IBM, Microsoft Azure and Oracle, the bank said. 

Growth to date has largely been organic, in the form of client wins, expansion of existing accounts, and new services being added to the stable.

"We also like current client contracted commitments to 2030 in the vicinity of ~$27b. This means the bulk of our forecast revenue growth through to 2030 is already locked in," said Macquarie. 

The bank expected revenues to double annually (on a CAGR basis) over the next 3 years, well ahead of the sector at ~40%.

The price target of $56 is based on equal-weighted EV/Sales (11-13x vs data centers' 12x) and EV/EBITDA (14-17x vs data centers' ~20x); hyperscalers averaged ~17x CY24 and pre-Covid, arguably more 'normal' cycles unencumbered by recent volatility.

CoreWeave got an average rating of 3.76 out of 5 by the sell side. Meanwhile, SA analysts largely recommended the company as a Hold.

data.title: Fiverr anticipates 30% revenue from services with AI-driven growth in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Fiverr International Ltd. (FVRR) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Micha Kaufman highlighted strong performance in Q1 2025, with revenue reaching $107.2 million, reflecting a 15% year-over-year growth. Adjusted EBITDA was $19.4 million with an 18% margin. Free cash flow grew 31.6% year-over-year to $27.4 million. Strategic initiatives included expanding Fiverr Pro and launching Fiverr Go.
  • Kaufman emphasized Fiverr Pro’s success in enterprise solutions, citing significant deals like a $200,000 engagement with a book publishing company and a collaboration with an online education company transitioning their YouTube production to Fiverr.
  • Kaufman noted the strong initial traction of Fiverr Go, which has seen 6,000 sellers and 200,000 buyers engage with the platform. The tool demonstrated a 56% uplift in one-hour conversions and a 10% increase in 14-day conversions for sellers using the Personal Assistant feature.
  • CFO Ofer Katz reported Marketplace revenue of $77.7 million and Services revenue of $29.5 million, the latter representing 27.5% of total revenue and projected to exceed 30% for the full year.
  • Katz announced an additional $100 million authorized for the stock repurchase program, signaling confidence in Fiverr’s long-term growth prospects.

Outlook

  • The company raised its full-year revenue guidance to $425 million to $438 million, reflecting 9% to 12% growth. Adjusted EBITDA is projected at $84 million to $90 million, targeting a 20% margin at the midpoint.
  • For Q2 2025, Fiverr expects revenue between $105 million and $109 million, with adjusted EBITDA of $20 million to $22 million.
  • Katz outlined plans to sustain marketing efficiency and moderate R&D expense growth for the remainder of the year.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 revenue was $107.2 million, up from $103.7 million in Q4 2024. Adjusted EBITDA increased to $19.4 million from $20.7 million in Q4, with a margin improvement of 100 basis points year-over-year.
  • Marketplace revenue was supported by a 27.7% take rate, while Services revenue grew 94% year-over-year, driven by Fiverr Ads, Seller Plus, and AutoDS.
  • Active buyers totaled 3.5 million, with an average spend per buyer of $309.

Q&A

  • Ronald Josey (Citi) questioned the progress of Fiverr Go and macroeconomic influences. Kaufman expressed satisfaction with Fiverr Go’s early performance, describing it as a conversion and confidence tool. He noted stable freelancing demand but highlighted the company’s ability to capitalize on opportunities.
  • Eric Sheridan (Goldman Sachs) inquired about the adoption of freelancing during macroeconomic volatility and margin trajectory. Katz emphasized the increasing role of freelancing in enterprise projects and projected continued marketing and R&D efficiency.
  • Jason Helfstein (Oppenheimer) asked about Services revenue growth sustainability. Katz affirmed its potential, forecasting Services to reach 30% of total revenue by year-end, supported by expanding offerings like Fiverr Go and AutoDS.
  • Joshua Chan (UBS) sought insights on larger client engagements and AutoDS. Kaufman and Katz explained the strategic focus on high-value buyers and clarified that AutoDS operates as a subscription-based model, unaffected by tariffs.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a cautiously positive tone, focusing on the scalability of Fiverr Go and macroeconomic impacts on growth.
  • Management maintained a confident stance, emphasizing strong execution and strategic advancements. Kaufman highlighted the transformative potential of Fiverr Go and its alignment with broader market needs, while Katz reaffirmed fiscal discipline and growth prospects.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone reflected increased optimism, driven by successful product launches and significant enterprise deals.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue grew from $103.7 million in Q4 2024 to $107.2 million in Q1 2025. Adjusted EBITDA saw a slight decrease but maintained a strong margin.
  • Services revenue’s contribution to total revenue increased, consistent with the company’s diversification strategy.
  • Management’s confidence grew, bolstered by early success in Fiverr Go and Fiverr Pro initiatives.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted towards scalability and the impact of macroeconomic conditions on enterprise adoption.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged stable but not improving macroeconomic conditions, which may limit Marketplace revenue growth.
  • Analysts raised questions about scalability and adoption of Fiverr Go across broader categories.
  • No immediate macroeconomic uplift was reported, maintaining exposure to external economic risks.

Final Takeaway

Fiverr delivered strong Q1 2025 results, driven by robust revenue growth and expansion in Services revenue. Strategic initiatives like Fiverr Go and Fiverr Pro have gained traction, demonstrating their potential to enhance conversion rates and enterprise engagement. With raised guidance for 2025 and a focus on innovation and efficiency, Fiverr is well-positioned for sustained growth despite macroeconomic challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Rio Tinto extracts first gallium from alumina refining in partnership with Indium
data.description:
Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) said Wednesday it extracted its first primary gallium as part of a partnership with Indium Corp. that aims to eventually produce commercial quantities of the critical, rare earth metal from bauxite processed at Rio's Vaudreuil alumina refinery in Quebec.

The preliminary step was performed at Indium's research and development facility located in Rome, New York, with the next phase of the project involving the assessment of extraction techniques to enable the production of larger quantities of gallium at pilot-scale.

If successful, Rio (NYSE:RIO) said it plans to build a demonstration plant in Saguenay-Lac-Saint-Jean, financially supported by the government of Quebec, with a capacity of up to 3.5 metric tons/year of gallium.

If Rio (RIO) succeeds in taking production to commercial scale, the miner anticipates output of as much as 40 metric tons/year, representing 5%-10% of the current global gallium production.

The news comes as China restricts exports of metals such as gallium, germanium and antimony to the U.S. as part of the trade war between the two countries.

data.title: Kinross gold targets $650M in shareholder returns for 2025 through dividends and buybacks
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Kinross Gold Corporation (KGC) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Paul Rollinson highlighted strong Q1 performance with production of 512,000 ounces, driven by Tasiast and Paracatu, which accounted for over half of total production and cash flow. He confirmed that Tasiast remains on track to meet its 2025 production guidance despite a fire-related shutdown in April.
  • Rollinson announced progress in growth projects, including underground development at Round Mountain Phase X, resource drilling at Curlew, and baseline studies at Lobo-Marte. Great Bear also advanced with detailed engineering and exploration.
  • CFO Andrea Freeborough reported Q1 adjusted earnings of $0.30 per share and free cash flow of $371 million. She emphasized lower costs of sales and all-in sustaining costs compared to the prior quarter. The company repurchased $60 million in shares to date and aims to return $650 million in capital to shareholders in 2025.
  • Freeborough noted improvements in the balance sheet, with net debt reduced to $540 million and a net debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 0.2 times. The company plans to repurchase $500 million in shares this year, supported by strong cash flow.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed full-year production guidance of 2 million ounces at a cost of sales of $1,120 per ounce and all-in sustaining costs of $1,500 per ounce. CapEx guidance remains at $1.15 billion.
  • Freeborough stated that operating costs are expected to rise due to changes in the accounting treatment for certain mining activities, inflation, and production sequencing. Inflation is projected at 3%-4% for the year.
  • Rollinson confirmed the reactivation of the share buyback program, targeting a minimum of $500 million in repurchases for 2025, alongside a quarterly dividend of $0.03 per share.

Financial Results

  • Q1 production totaled 512,000 gold equivalent ounces, with sales of 506,000 ounces. The cost of sales was $1,038 per ounce, and all-in sustaining costs were $1,355 per ounce.
  • Adjusted operating cash flow was $676 million, and free cash flow excluding working capital changes reached $472 million.
  • Tasiast achieved production of 138,000 ounces at $811 per ounce in costs, while Paracatu produced 147,000 ounces at $951 per ounce. La Coipa and U.S. assets met production and cost expectations.
  • The company ended Q1 with $695 million in cash and $2.3 billion in liquidity. The term loan was fully repaid during the quarter.

Q&A

  • Anita Soni, CIBC: Asked about Tasiast's restart timeline following the fire. COO Claude Schimper confirmed a three-week shutdown, including additional maintenance work.
  • Tanya Jakusconek, Scotiabank: Inquired about Great Bear's permitting progress. EVP Geoff Gold stated that all necessary permits for current activities are in place, with additional permits expected later in the year. Negotiations with First Nations remain constructive.
  • Carey MacRury, Canaccord Genuity: Questioned the share buyback program's dependency on gold prices. CFO Freeborough indicated strong commitment to the $500 million target under current market conditions.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts demonstrated a positive tone, commending strong Q1 results and management's clear guidance. They focused on operational resilience and shareholder returns.
  • Management displayed confidence in achieving guidance and advancing strategic projects. Rollinson emphasized the company's robust financial position and disciplined approach.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained consistent with a focus on operational excellence and capital returns.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Production in Q1 2025 was slightly higher than Q4 2024, supported by strong contributions from Tasiast and Paracatu.
  • Cost of sales and all-in sustaining costs were lower in Q1, reflecting higher production and cost efficiencies.
  • Management's tone remained optimistic, with continued focus on shareholder returns and growth initiatives. Analysts maintained a positive outlook, consistent with the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Potential risks include inflationary pressures, changes in accounting treatments increasing operating costs, and project execution challenges.
  • Analysts raised questions about permitting timelines for Great Bear and the operational impact of the Tasiast fire.

Final Takeaway

Kinross Gold Corporation delivered strong Q1 2025 results, supported by robust production and cost efficiencies. Management reaffirmed annual guidance and emphasized shareholder returns through dividends and a $500 million share buyback program. Progress in strategic growth projects, including Great Bear and Round Mountain Phase X, positions the company well for sustained future production. Analysts showed confidence in Kinross's operational and financial strategies, with management maintaining a disciplined approach to capital allocation and project execution.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Apple's Cue says iPhone maker exploring adding AI search as queries fall
data.description:
• Eddy Cue, Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) senior vice president of services, said on Tuesday that the company is exploring the option of adding artificial intelligence search options to its Safari web browser.
  • Cue, who made the comments at Google's (GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) antitrust trial, added that Perplexity AI and Anthropic, maker of the Claude chatbot, could be options. Searches in Apple's Safari browser fell for the first time in April, Cue added.
  • At stake is a significant chunk of high-margin revenue for Apple, with Google widely believed to have $20B to the iPhone maker in 2022 to be the default search option in Safari. 
  • As generative AI chatbots such as ChatGPT, Claude and others become more prevalent, some have expressed concern about the dominance of Google's search market share. During recent testimony at the trial, Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai said he expects to sign an agreement with Apple by the middle of this year to include its AI service Gemini on new iPhones.
  • Apple shares erased earlier gains and fell 0.5% following Cue's comments, while Google shares fell 4%.

data.title: CVS Health’s Q1 beat prompts Glenview Capital to lower stake
data.description:
Activist investor Glenview Capital Management has reduced its ownership stake in CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) after the pharmacy chain operator raised its full-year earnings outlook, posting better-than-expected Q1 2025 financials early this month.

The New York-based investment firm, which convinced CVS (NYSE:CVS) management to add four new members to the company’s board in November, said it has offloaded 3.75M CVS shares, indicating a reduction of a third of its stake.

“Our position size afforded us the scale necessary to constructively engage with the Board, Management, and fellow Shareholders in Q3 2024 on a multi-faceted performance improvement plan,” Glenview Capital said in a press release late Tuesday.

The firm added that CVS shares have returned 25% in total since its purchases in May 2024, “reflecting initial progress in the long-term cultural and operational turnaround underway at the company.”

“Our confidence in the near-, medium-, and long-term outlook for CVS remains strong,” the hedge fund noted, adding that CVS remains its largest position and the company doesn’t intend to make further adjustments to its holdings.
